body_content:
Published by Travelxploria on Sat Jun 14 2025

Kashmir, famously known as the “Paradise on Earth,” is a breathtakingly beautiful tourist destination in India. Surrounded by snow-capped mountains and serene lakes, this valley is famous for its natural beauty, rich cultural heritage, and unique blend of Indian, Persian, and Central Asian influences. In this blog, we’ll take you on an unforgettable Kashmir travel story and explore the beauty and wonder of this breathtakingly beautiful place.

Our Kashmir Travel Story Started

Day 1: Discovering Srinagar

Our Kashmir travel story started with a flight from Delhi to Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir. Upon arrival, we were immediately struck by the cool and crisp air of the valley and the picturesque view of the Dal Lake, surrounded by snow-capped mountains, that greeted us from a distance. After checking into our hotel, we headed straight to the famous Mughal Gardens.

Day 2: Exploring Mughal Gardens and Houseboats on Dal Lake

The Mughal Gardens, a group of terraced gardens built by the Mughal Emperors in Srinagar, are famous for their stunning views of the Dal Lake and the snow-capped mountains. The serenity and beauty of the gardens left us spellbound. In the evening, we embarked on a unique and memorable houseboat ride on Dal Lake, witnessing the everyday life of the local people who live on the lake in these houseboats.

Day 3: Visiting Shankaracharya Temple and Lal Chowk Market

The next day, we visited the famous Shankaracharya Temple, located on a hilltop in Srinagar. This temple is one of the oldest shrines in the region, dedicated to Lord Shiva and offering a panoramic view of the city. In the evening, we headed to the local market, Lal Chowk, to indulge in shopping and sample traditional Kashmiri cuisine.

Day 4: Adventure in Gulmarg

The following day, we headed to Gulmarg, a popular ski resort located about 55 km from Srinagar. The natural beauty and lush green meadows of Gulmarg left us speechless, and we went for a horseback ride to a nearby mountain, offering a panoramic view of the entire valley.

Day 5: Trekking in Pahalgam

Next, we visited Pahalgam, another popular tourist destination in Kashmir, known for its beautiful alpine meadows and scenic beauty. We went for a trek to the nearby mountains and were simply stunned by the natural beauty of the place, witnessing the famous snow-covered peaks of the Himalayan Mountains.

Day 6: Exploring Jama Masjid Mosque

On our last day in Kashmir, we visited Srinagar’s famous mosque, the Jama Masjid, which is one of the largest in India and a beautiful example of Islamic architecture. This mosque is considered one of the most important pilgrimage sites for Muslims.

Our Kashmir travel story was nothing short of magical. From the stunning beauty of the Mughal Gardens and the serene Dal Lake to the rich cultural heritage of the Jama Masjid mosque, every moment was filled with wonder and awe. Kashmir is a must-visit destination for anyone looking to experience the magic of India.

Day 7: Visit to Sonamarg

Our journey to Kashmir continued with a visit to the picturesque town of Sonamarg, which is located about 83 km from Srinagar. Sonamarg, which means “Meadow of Gold,” is famous for its natural beauty, and it lived up to its name. We were surrounded by majestic mountains, glaciers, and alpine meadows, which were simply breathtaking. We went for a trek in the surrounding hills and took in the stunning views of the valley.

Day 8: Trekking in the Himalayas

Next, we headed to the Himalayan Mountains for a challenging but rewarding trek. The trek took us through lush forests and beautiful meadows, and we were able to witness the stunning beauty of the Himalayas up close and personal. The trek was truly a once-in-a-lifetime experience, and we were grateful for the opportunity to explore the beauty of the region in this way.

Day 9: Srinagar’s Historic Sites

On our final day in Kashmir, we explored some of Srinagar’s most famous historical sites. We visited the Pari Mahal, which is an ancient palace built by the Mughal Emperors, and the Hari Parbat Fort, which is a famous fort in the city that offers stunning views of the valley. We also visited Nagin Lake, which is a popular tourist destination in Srinagar, and we took a stroll around the nearby gardens and parks.

Day 10: Zanskar Valley and Kargil

One of the most offbeat destinations in Kashmir is the Zanskar Valley, located in the remote region of Ladakh. The valley is known for its stunning scenic beauty and its rich cultural heritage, which is a unique blend of Tibetan and Indian influences. During our visit to the valley, we trekked to the remote villages, where we got to experience the traditional way of life of the local people.

Next, we headed to Kargil, which is located in the heart of Ladakh and is known for its rich cultural heritage and scenic beauty. We visited the famous Kargil War Memorial, which is dedicated to the Indian soldiers who lost their lives during the Kargil War. The memorial offers a panoramic view of the surrounding mountains and is a must-visit destination for anyone interested in history and military heritage.

Conclusion Of Our Kashmir Travel Story

Our journey to Kashmir was an unforgettable experience that we will cherish for a lifetime. From the stunning natural beauty of the valley to the rich cultural heritage of the region, we were amazed by the beauty and wonder of this “Paradise on Earth.” We highly recommend Team Travelxploria one of the best travel agency in Kolkata they made our journey much easier, and a visit to Kashmir are much for anyone looking for a truly magical travel experience.

Kashmir travel story has truly been a journey to remember. The valley is surrounded by snow-capped mountains, serene lakes, and lush green meadows, and it is famous for its natural beauty, rich cultural heritage, and unique blend of Indian, Persian, and Central Asian influences. With its stunning views, historic sites, and amazing trekking opportunities, Kashmir is truly a must-visit destination for anyone looking to experience the magic of India.
